E-Rod: season ERA 5.02, K/9 8.35, BB/9 3.50, WHIP 1.54 — SMALL SAMPLE (1 start), recent ERA 0.0 from single start. ERA gap of 1.03 favors Holmes but under 2.0 threshold. No reliable splits available for either arm.
picks.lineup_analysis: Atlanta OPS 0.935 — elite offense. Arizona OPS 0.851 — solid. Gap of 0.084 is under the 0.100 threshold for a rubric point but Atlanta's lineup is a genuine run-scoring threat against E-Rod's 5.02 ERA and a fatigued Arizona bullpen.
picks.bullpen_note: Arizona SEVERE: 4 fatigued arms (Andrew Hoffmann, Kevin Ginkel, Joe Ross, James McCann) — significant late-inning run-scoring risk. Atlanta MODERATE: 1 arm (Osvaldo Bido) — manageable.
picks.umpire_note: TBD umpire — using league average 8.8 rpg, 22% K rate. Neutral. No directional lean applied.
picks.park_note: Chase Field dome, runs factor 1.02, HR factor 1.04. Neutral environment — no weather interference.
picks.weather_impact: Dome — weather irrelevant. No wind impact.
picks.key_edge: Arizona bullpen SEVERE with 4 fatigued arms facing Atlanta's elite OPS 0.935 offense behind shaky E-Rod (ERA 5.02) — late-inning run-scoring elevated.
picks.rationale: Eduardo Rodriguez carries the worst ERA on the board at 5.02 and will hand a SEVERE bullpen (4 arms used heavily) to a lineup posting .935 OPS. The combination of a poor starting pitcher and deeply fatigued Arizona relief corps creates genuine OVER pressure even in a dome with a neutral park. However, only 2 rubric points accumulate (SEVERE bullpen +2, all other factors neutral), and the EV sits near the minimum threshold at approximately 4%, warranting a WATCH rather than an active bet.
picks.avoid_reason: Only 2 rubric points accumulated — below Tier C minimum of 4-5 points. Both SPs are SMALL SAMPLE (1 start each), recent form unreliable. EV borderline at ~4%. Track for line movement toward 9.5 which would sharpen the UNDER or confirm OVER steam.
picks.flags: Both SPs have SMALL SAMPLE flags (1 start each) — ERA figures are primarily 2025 data. HP umpire TBD.

picks.game: New York Mets @ San Francisco Giants
picks.sp_analysis: McLean: season ERA 2.06, K/9 10.69, BB/9 3.0, WHIP 1.04 — elite strikeout pitcher, SMALL SAMPLE (1 start) so recent ERA 3.6 not used as confirming factor. BB/9 3.0 meets threshold. K/9 10.69 well above 8.5 NRFI threshold. Mahle: season ERA 2.18, K/9 6.89, BB/9 3.03, WHIP 1.13 — SMALL SAMPLE (1 start). Home ERA split 4.5 / K/9 11.25 from 1 start (unreliable small sample). Season K/9 6.89 is below 8.5 NRFI ideal threshold — flagged as partial concern. BB/9 3.03 barely above 3.0 threshold.

picks.bullpen_note: Mets bullpen SEVERE: 2 arms (Sean Manaea, Tobias Myers) heavily used. Giants bullpen SEVERE: 2 arms (Blade Tidwell, José Buttó) heavily used. Both bullpens are fatigued but NRFI resolves after top of first inning — bullpen fatigue is irrelevant to this specific prop.

picks.park_note: Oracle Park: runs factor 0.90 (below 0.92 threshold — +1 rubric point for pitcher-friendly lean), HR factor 0.78. Historically suppresses run scoring. Cold air at 49°F further depresses offense.
picks.weather_impact: 12.2 mph NNE crosswind — no directional scoring impact per wind_impact field. Temp 49°F — cold air suppresses scoring, consistent with NRFI lean.
picks.nrfi_analysis: NRFI probability: 80% vs YRFI 20%. Model fair price -400; book offering -135 (implied 57.4%). Conservative haircut to 72% NRFI win prob still yields EV of ~15%. McLean NRFI score 1.437 (elite), Mahle NRFI score 1.228 (strong). Book source confirmed. Oracle Park runs factor 0.90 and 49°F cold air further support scoreless first inning. Caution: Mahle season K/9 6.89 is below the 8.5 ideal NRFI threshold — this is why conservative win prob of 72% is used rather than the model's 80%.
picks.key_edge: Model prices NRFI fair value at -400 while book offers -135 — a massive 22+ point EV gap. Even at a deeply conservative 72% win probability, EV is approximately 15%.
picks.rationale: The book is severely mispricing this NRFI, offering -135 on a prop the model values at -400 (80% probability). Oracle Park is one of the most pitcher-friendly venues in baseball (runs factor 0.90), and cold 49°F air further suppresses first-inning run scoring. McLean's elite K/9 of 10.69 and NRFI score of 1.437 make him particularly dangerous to opposing lineups in the opening frame, and Mahle's NRFI score of 1.228 is also strong despite his lower season K/9. Applying a significant conservative haircut to 72% still produces ~15% EV, clearing the 5% NRFI threshold comfortably — this is the highest-confidence bet on the two-game slate.
picks.flags: Both SPs SMALL SAMPLE (1 start each). HP umpire TBD. Mahle season K/9 6.89 below 8.5 NRFI ideal threshold — factored into conservative win prob adjustment. Both team OPS values missing from data — lineup strength unassessable.
